comment: Arrakis — a Dune-style detuned sub-octave drone. Pitches the input down one or two octaves and splits it into two voices detuned in OPPOSITE directions by a slowly-swept amount, so they beat against each other and the throb speeds up / slows down (the 'two low oscillators tuning back and forth' sound). Saturation + low-pass add the massive weight. Desktop preview implements the full DSP; pedal C port is staged (audio_nop smoke build).
fxid_comment: 0x01C5 = above the repo's used range, distinct from microloom (450)/flower (451)/shatter (452). Verify uniqueness before flashing.

params.name: Detune
params.comment: Spread between the two voices in cents (the beat amount). 0 = locked, high = wide wobble.

params.name: Sweep
params.comment: How fast the detune wanders back and forth (~0.02..0.6 Hz). The 'tuning back and forth'.

params.name: Drive
params.comment: Saturation / weight. Adds harmonics and the brassy Dune growl.

params.name: Tone
params.comment: Low-pass cutoff. Low = dark and massive, high = more upper growl.

audio_nop_comment: Pedal C port starts as a UI-only smoke build. Full DSP validated on desktop first; see tools/audio_preview/renderers/arrakis.py.
